Date of Crash: 03/13/2023      Time: 16:42 hrs       Day: Mon
Location: Causeway Blvd at Bermuda Ave (T-intersection), Tampa, FL 33619
V-2 driver Reyes complained of neck and lower-back pain on scene; transported non-emergency by HCFR to Tampa General. Coded "Possible Injury (C)."
V-1 driver Aliyah cited under FSS 316.0895(1) — Following Too Closely.

V-1: Improper following distance (primary). No alcohol, no drugs. Not distracted per driver statement (hands-free only; no call active).
V-2: None coded.

evidence_found.text_excerpt: I was returning to the terminal. That's, uh, that's the protocol. Log the truck in, do the post-trip inspection, drop the keys. Before I, you know, before I clock out.

evidence_found.text_excerpt: Yes ma'am. Causeway is the route back to the terminal. There's not really another way to get there from the Adamo warehouse, unless you wanted to go all the way around on the interstate, which, you know, nobody does.

evidence_found.locator: Recorded Statement — Aliyah re: load status
evidence_found.text_excerpt: Empty. Or, uh, mostly empty. I had a couple of, uh, empty pallets in the back from one of the stops. That was it.

evidence_found.locator: Recorded Statement — Aliyah re: phone use
evidence_found.text_excerpt: No ma'am. Hands-free Bluetooth is the only thing we're allowed to do, and I wasn't even on a call. I was just driving.

assessments.claim_text: Driver Marcus Aliyah was an authorized, scheduled driver operating within the scope of his assigned duties at the time of loss.
assessments.reasoning: Aliyah is the assigned driver of Unit 14 for the 03/13/2023 BR-Mon-A route per the dispatch log, with no reassignment or swap noted. The dispatch log explicitly states that vehicle use during the return leg is on-duty per Northwind operations manual §4.3. Aliyah's own statement confirms he was returning the truck to the terminal per protocol. The incident occurred at 16:42, within his 07:30–17:00 shift window. A trivial residual uncertainty is retained for any undisclosed deviation or personal errand not captured in the file documents.

assessments.claim_text: Driver Aliyah meets the policy's driver eligibility requirements (valid CDL, age, and experience).
assessments.reasoning: The declarations page requires a valid CDL Class B (at minimum) and age 23+ with three years commercial driving experience. DOC-004 shows Aliyah holds CDL #A2241-FL expiring 2024-08-31 (not expired at loss date). DOC-002 lists the same CDL. Aliyah stated he has been driving for Northwind for approximately four years as of March 2023, satisfying the experience requirement. His DOB per DOC-002 is 12/04/1975, making him 47 at the time of loss, well above the age threshold. Note: the declarations reference CDL Class B as the minimum for Unit 14; the dispatch log shows CDL class prefix 'A2241' suggesting a Class A license, which meets or exceeds a Class B requirement. A small residual is kept pending formal MVR confirmation.

The crash is a straightforward rear-end collision caused by Unit 14. The contributing factor is entirely attributable to V-1. No indication in the file of any coverage defense relating to the nature of the injury itself.

assessments.claim_text: There are no apparent exclusions or policy defenses that would bar coverage for this BI exposure.
assessments.reasoning: The file does not reveal: (1) any intentional act; (2) any pollution/cargo exclusion trigger; (3) any use outside the policy's classification (pickup/delivery, radius ≤ 200 mi — the Brandon corridor is well within Tampa area); (4) any alcohol or drug involvement; (5) any unauthorized vehicle use. The deductible on BI is $0. The UM rejection is noted but irrelevant to a first-party BI analysis against the named insured's own policy for a third-party claimant. The 8% residual accounts for: (a) the MVR not yet confirmed in file; (b) any policy condition breach (e.g., late notice) not yet assessed; (c) any potential exclusion language in the CA 00 01 form not reproduced in these documents that could be argued in an edge scenario.

outcomes.claim_text: Clean Coverage — policy responds to Reyes BI claim without reservation.
outcomes.reasoning: All primary coverage prerequisites are satisfied on the available record: policy in force, Unit 14 is a scheduled covered auto under Symbol 7, Aliyah is the authorized on-duty driver within classification, the loss falls within the policy period, the BI arises from a covered accident, the deductible is $0, and no exclusions are apparent in the file. The strength of the evidence across all four documents is internally consistent and corroborated (police report, dispatch log, and recorded statement all align). The 12% not assigned here is split between ROR and denial.

would_shift_distribution: Confirmation of a current, valid CDL for Aliyah via formal MVR pull would increase clean coverage probability.
Discovery that Aliyah's CDL was suspended or revoked at the time of loss would shift mass toward ROR or denial.
Evidence of a personal deviation or detour prior to the return-to-terminal leg not captured in dispatch log would shift toward ROR.

Receipt and review of the full CA 00 01 policy form and all endorsements (particularly any driver exclusion endorsements not listed on the declarations page) could shift distribution in either direction.
Evidence of late notice by the named insured or a breach of cooperation condition would shift toward ROR.
Medical records confirming the nature and extent of Reyes's injuries would not affect coverage determination but are relevant to reserving and valuation.

coverage_analysis_memo.body:
COVERAGE ANALYSIS MEMORANDUM

File No.: CLM-2023-04471
Exposure: EXP-2023-04471-REYES-BI
Policy: NWL-CA-024471 (CA 00 01 ISO Commercial Auto)
Named Insured: Northwind Logistics, LLC
Claimant: Janet M. Reyes
Loss Date: 03/13/2023
Prepared: 2025-01-31

─────────────────────────────────────────────
I. COVERAGE FRAMEWORK
─────────────────────────────────────────────
Policy NWL-CA-024471 is an ISO CA 00 01 Commercial Automobile policy issued to Northwind Logistics, LLC. The policy period runs from 01/01/2023 to 01/01/2024 (DOC-001). The loss date of 03/13/2023 is within the policy period. Liability coverage is written on a Combined Single Limit of $1,000,000 per accident with a $0 deductible (DOC-001).

Covered autos are written under Symbol 7 — Specifically Described Autos. Unit 14, the 2019 Freightliner M2-106, is scheduled on the policy with Liability, Comprehensive, and Collision coverage (DOC-001).

─────────────────────────────────────────────
II. VEHICLE — COVERED AUTO STATUS
─────────────────────────────────────────────
Unit 14 is expressly listed in the Schedule of Covered Autos as: "2019 Freightliner M2-106, VIN 1FVACWDT9KHKL4218, GVWR 24,500 lbs, Body: Box truck (24-ft), Garage: Tampa terminal, Class 2-D-T-1 (radius ≤ 200 mi, secondary class T — Truck), Use: pickup/delivery — general commodities. Liability + Comp + Collision" (DOC-001). The police report independently confirms: "V-1: 2019 Freightliner M2-106 (24-ft box truck, no trailer), tag FL-TRK-Q4218. Owner: Northwind Logistics LLC. Insurer: per policy NWL-CA-024471" (DOC-002). Covered auto status: confirmed.

─────────────────────────────────────────────
III. DRIVER — AUTHORIZATION AND ELIGIBILITY
─────────────────────────────────────────────
The dispatch log confirms: "Marcus Aliyah is the assigned driver of Unit 14 for the 2023-03-13 BR-Mon-A route. No reassignment, no swap." (DOC-004). The vehicle use during the return leg is characterized as on-duty per Northwind operations manual §4.3 (DOC-004).

The declarations page requires: "All scheduled drivers must hold a valid CDL Class B for Unit 14. Annual MVR pull by Producer. Drivers must be 23 years of age or older with three years documented commercial driving experience" (DOC-001). The dispatch log reflects CDL #A2241-FL with expiration 2024-08-31 (DOC-004), which had not expired at the time of loss. The police report lists Aliyah's DOB as 12/04/1975 (age 47 at loss) and the same CDL number (DOC-002). Aliyah stated he has been driving for Northwind for approximately four years (DOC-003), satisfying the experience threshold on its face.

Note: The formal MVR document has not been produced in this file. The annual MVR pull is a policy condition managed by the Producer. Eligibility appears consistent with available evidence, but formal MVR confirmation is outstanding.

─────────────────────────────────────────────
IV. PERMISSIVE USE / SCOPE OF EMPLOYMENT
─────────────────────────────────────────────
At the time of impact (16:42), Aliyah was returning Unit 14 to the Causeway terminal after completing the final delivery on route BR-Mon-A (final drop logged 15:51, DOC-004). The dispatch log records: "Truck inbound to terminal at time of incident; ETA to terminal was 17:00 per route plan. Post-trip inspection / unit lock-in not yet performed (incident occurred prior to scheduled check-in)" (DOC-004). Aliyah's recorded statement corroborates: "I was returning to the terminal. That's, uh, that's the protocol. Log the truck in, do the post-trip inspection, drop the keys. Before I, you know, before I clock out" (DOC-003). He further confirmed: "Yes ma'am. Causeway is the route back to the terminal. There's not really another way to get there from the Adamo warehouse" (DOC-003). The incident location (Causeway Blvd at Bermuda Ave, Tampa, FL 33619) is consistent with the return route and the named insured's terminal address (4218 Causeway Blvd, Tampa, FL 33619, per DOC-001). The return-to-terminal leg constitutes permissive, on-duty use.

─────────────────────────────────────────────
V. NATURE OF LOSS / OCCURRENCE
─────────────────────────────────────────────
The police report documents a front-to-rear collision: V-1 (Unit 14, Aliyah) struck the rear of V-2 (Reyes) while V-2 was decelerating to turn right at the Bermuda Ave T-intersection (DOC-002). Contributing factor for V-1: "Improper following distance (primary). No alcohol, no drugs" (DOC-002). V-1 was cited for FSS 316.0895(1) — Following Too Closely (DOC-002). No contributing factors were coded against V-2 (DOC-002). The occurrence is a straightforward covered auto accident with no apparent excluded causes.

─────────────────────────────────────────────
VI. CLAIMANT INJURY
─────────────────────────────────────────────
Reyes was transported non-emergency to Tampa General and coded "Possible Injury (C)" (DOC-002). Medical records and treatment history have not yet been produced in this file and will be required for reserving and valuation purposes. No coverage-based concern is presented by the injury characterization at this stage.

─────────────────────────────────────────────
VII. APPLICABLE LIMIT AND DEDUCTIBLE
─────────────────────────────────────────────
The applicable coverage part is CP-NWL-2023-BI. The Combined Single Limit is $1,000,000 per accident. The deductible on liability is $0. No SIR applies.

─────────────────────────────────────────────
VIII. POTENTIAL COVERAGE ISSUES / ITEMS REQUIRING FOLLOW-UP
─────────────────────────────────────────────
1. MVR Confirmation: The formal annual MVR for Aliyah has not been produced. The dispatch log references CDL #A2241-FL (exp. 2024-08-31). The Producer should be asked to confirm the MVR is current and on file.

2. Full Policy Form Review: The complete CA 00 01 form and all endorsements were not reproduced among the documents reviewed. A full exclusion review should be performed against the policy jacket.

3. Notice and Cooperation: The file does not contain a formal notice analysis. The incident was reported on 03/13/2023 and appears to have been reported to the carrier promptly (dispatch log same day; police report 03/14/2023; recorded statement 03/15/2023). No breach is apparent but formal notice compliance should be confirmed.

4. Medical Records: Reyes's treatment records from Tampa General and any follow-up providers are needed for reserve evaluation.

─────────────────────────────────────────────
IX. COVERAGE PROBABILITY DISTRIBUTION
─────────────────────────────────────────────
Clean Coverage: 0.88
Reservation of Rights: 0.10
Denial: 0.02

The distribution reflects that all primary coverage triggers are satisfied on the present record, with a minority probability allocated to ROR pending MVR confirmation and full policy form review. The denial probability is minimal given the absence of any identified exclusionary basis in the file.

─────────────────────────────────────────────
NOTE: This memo presents the coverage specialist's analysis and probability assessments. It does not constitute a coverage position or recommendation. All coverage determinations remain with the assigned adjuster and supervising counsel.

ror_letter.body:
RESERVATION OF RIGHTS LETTER
[DRAFT — FOR ADJUSTER REVIEW ONLY — NOT YET ISSUED]

[DATE]

Northwind Logistics, LLC
4218 Causeway Blvd
Tampa, FL 33619

Re:   Claim No.:       CLM-2023-04471
      Policy No.:      NWL-CA-024471
      Date of Loss:    March 13, 2023
      Claimant:        Janet M. Reyes
      Vehicle:         2019 Freightliner M2-106, Unit 14

Dear Northwind Logistics, LLC:

This letter is written on behalf of [Carrier] in connection with the above-referenced claim arising from a motor vehicle accident that occurred on March 13, 2023, on Causeway Boulevard at Bermuda Avenue, Tampa, Florida. You have reported that Marcus Aliyah, operating your Unit 14 (2019 Freightliner M2-106), was involved in a rear-end collision with a 2022 Honda Civic operated by Janet M. Reyes, who has asserted a claim for bodily injury.

[Carrier] is conducting a coverage investigation under Policy Number NWL-CA-024471, a Commercial Automobile Policy (ISO CA 00 01 form) issued to Northwind Logistics, LLC, for the policy period January 1, 2023 to January 1, 2024. [Carrier] has undertaken the investigation of this claim, including obtaining an initial recorded statement from Mr. Aliyah and reviewing the Hillsborough County Sheriff's Office traffic crash report. [Carrier] wishes to proceed with the defense, adjustment, and investigation of this matter while expressly reserving all rights under the policy and applicable law.

I. BASIS FOR THIS RESERVATION

The policy's Driver Eligibility provision states: "All scheduled drivers must hold a valid CDL Class B for Unit 14. Annual MVR pull by Producer. Drivers must be 23 years of age or older with three years documented commercial driving experience." While Mr. Aliyah's CDL number and expiration date have been identified in the dispatch records (CDL #A2241-FL, exp. 2024-08-31), the formal Motor Vehicle Record (MVR) that the policy requires to be maintained on file by the Producer has not yet been produced for review. [Carrier] reserves its rights pending confirmation that Mr. Aliyah's license was valid and in good standing at the time of loss and that all driver eligibility conditions were satisfied.

Additionally, [Carrier]'s review of the complete CA 00 01 policy form, endorsements, and all applicable conditions is ongoing. [Carrier] reserves its rights with respect to any policy conditions, exclusions, or requirements that may be identified upon completion of that review, including but not limited to notice and cooperation provisions.

II. RESERVATION

[Carrier] expressly reserves all rights, defenses, and remedies available under the policy and applicable law, including but not limited to the right to disclaim coverage, deny the claim, or withdraw from any defense, if further investigation reveals that coverage is barred or limited. This reservation of rights is not an admission that coverage does or does not exist, and [Carrier] does not waive any policy term, condition, exclusion, or defense by proceeding with its investigation or by issuing this letter.

III. YOUR COOPERATION IS REQUIRED

The policy requires your full cooperation with [Carrier]'s investigation. We ask that you promptly:

1. Request that Gulf Coast Commercial Brokerage, Inc. produce the annual MVR on file for Marcus Aliyah for the policy year 2023 and forward it to [Carrier].
2. Provide any additional documentation concerning Mr. Aliyah's driving record, qualifications, and employment records relating to his CDL status.
3. Preserve all documents, electronic records, and communications relating to this incident.

IV. INDEPENDENT COUNSEL

Because [Carrier] is defending this claim under a reservation of rights, you may have the right to retain independent counsel at your own expense. You are encouraged to consult with an attorney of your choice regarding your rights and obligations under these circumstances.

Please do not hesitate to contact us if you have any questions.

Sincerely,

[Claims Specialist]
[Carrier Name]
[Address]
[Phone / Email]

─────────────────────────────────────────────
NOTE TO ADJUSTER: This letter is a draft for review purposes only. Issuance requires adjuster and supervising counsel review and approval. The specific coverage questions to be reserved should be confirmed against the complete policy form before issuance. This draft does not constitute a coverage position or recommendation.

denial_letter.body:
COVERAGE DENIAL LETTER
[DRAFT — FOR ADJUSTER REVIEW ONLY — NOT YET ISSUED]
[LOW PROBABILITY PATH — 0.02 — SEE COVERAGE MEMO FOR CONTEXT]

[DATE]

Northwind Logistics, LLC
4218 Causeway Blvd
Tampa, FL 33619

Re:   Claim No.:       CLM-2023-04471
      Policy No.:      NWL-CA-024471
      Date of Loss:    March 13, 2023
      Claimant:        Janet M. Reyes
      Vehicle:         2019 Freightliner M2-106, Unit 14

Dear Northwind Logistics, LLC:

This letter is written on behalf of [Carrier] regarding the above-referenced claim. [Carrier] has completed its initial coverage investigation and, based on the ground(s) described below, is advising that coverage may not be available under Policy Number NWL-CA-024471 for the reasons set forth herein.

[NOTE TO ADJUSTER: This denial draft is provided as a placeholder for the low-probability denial scenario. On the present record, no adequate basis for denial has been identified. This letter SHOULD NOT BE ISSUED unless further investigation produces affirmative evidence of a coverage bar — for example, confirmation that Marcus Aliyah's CDL was suspended or revoked at the time of loss in violation of the policy's driver eligibility condition, evidence of a material misrepresentation in the application, or the identification of an applicable exclusion in the complete CA 00 01 policy form. The following template should be revised to reflect the specific ground(s) identified.]

I. POLICY AND CLAIM SUMMARY

[Carrier] issued Commercial Automobile Policy No. NWL-CA-024471 to Northwind Logistics, LLC for the policy period January 1, 2023 to January 1, 2024. The policy was written on ISO form CA 00 01 with covered autos under Symbol 7 — Specifically Described Autos.

Unit 14 is listed on the Schedule of Covered Autos as: "2019 Freightliner M2-106, VIN 1FVACWDT9KHKL4218 ... Use: pickup/delivery — general commodities. Liability + Comp + Collision."

On March 13, 2023, Unit 14, operated by Marcus Aliyah, was involved in a rear-end collision on Causeway Boulevard, Tampa, FL, resulting in a bodily injury claim by Janet M. Reyes.

II. GROUNDS FOR DENIAL
[TO BE COMPLETED UPON IDENTIFICATION OF SPECIFIC COVERAGE BAR]

Example — Driver Eligibility (if MVR reveals CDL invalidity):
The policy requires: "All scheduled drivers must hold a valid CDL Class B for Unit 14. Annual MVR pull by Producer. Drivers must be 23 years of age or older with three years documented commercial driving experience." (DOC-001). If investigation confirms that Mr. Aliyah's CDL was not valid and in good standing at the time of loss, this condition precedent would not be satisfied and coverage would not be available for this occurrence.

III. RIGHTS RESERVED

[Carrier] reserves all additional rights, defenses, and remedies available under the policy and applicable law. Nothing in this letter shall be construed as a waiver of any policy term, condition, exclusion, or other defense.

IV. YOUR RIGHTS

You may have rights under Florida law to dispute this determination. You are encouraged to consult with an attorney of your choosing. You may also have the right to submit a complaint to the Florida Department of Financial Services, Division of Consumer Services.

Sincerely,
